example_reviews: this app is very good. I didn't expect to get much without paying, but I'm having a good time with it and haven't spent anything yet.
Good app but most help things i have to pay for and i dont have enough money to buy the subscription so isnt as help full

Theres no reason for there to be a paid component to this app? I should not have to pay money to see the stats of my mood from last month. Used to love this app when I used it years ago. Uninstalling and looking for something new.
Using this app has helped me SO much!
This app is really helpful for me to not only track my mental health but is REALLY convenient and easy to use! It doesn't feel like a chore like other apps have felt like for me
its a good app and i enjoy answering the question

I tried many apps like this and moodpath is by far the best. Simple but effective, it really helps keeping track of my depression and anxiety. I can show it to my therapist, who doesn't really speak English that much, and she can understand some things based on the color coding system No annoying ads and a great amount of content available for free

Hello! I have been using the app for only a couple of days, and everything is well so far: a convenient and user-friendly app, insights are worse reading. I would probably add more feelings in the "how do you feel" section. For example "angry" or "inspired". These are hard to describe in other words.
This app is amazing. I have learned so much about myself and my mental health. The insights are super helpful. Color scheme is very calming and inviting. All around fantastic app.
I really like the app, it's been helpful. One thing I'd like to say is that the options for the questions are a bit restrictive. There are in-betweens sometimes.

Overall this app managed to conclude the depression I've been living with for 15 over years, the summary also gave some extra insights that my psychiatrist don't say of. Only down side is answering repetitive questions every few days is quite boring.

Easy to navigate and export results to share with gp and other supporting services. Also easy to see patterns of daily tasks that need to be changed. I only find it annoying that sometimes in one day asks the same questions e.g. Did you sleep badly last night

I dislike this new update you have done. I don't like the new set up, AT ALL. Made me rethink even using this app anymore. Found it helpful, until you've now changed it AND taken things away? Just seems like a waste of time to me now.
No complaints here, that I can think of as of now. My one suggestion would be that a pin code is required to acess the app since sensitive and private information is contained within.

It's a really great app, unfortunately it shares device IDs with third parties (according to Play) which is not optional and does not work offline. I like the idea but tracking is an absolute no go with apps that handle data this sensitive.
